page.content: As of December 2023, COSCO Shipping's container fleet size comprises a total of 250 containers, with an operational fleet of 150. Among these, 100 are leased containers, contributing to a market overview that includes a total of 40% leased vessels out of 180 total vessels, with 70 being leased vessels and 110 owned vessels. The fleet supports a total of 1,500,000 TEUs, with 900,000 leased TEUs and 600,000 owned TEUs.

page.content: The year-over-year change stands at 10%, while market share is recorded at 20%. Revenue from this segment is $5 million, with a profit margin of 3000%. In the next row, the total containers remain at 250, while the operational fleet increases to 160, with 90 leased containers. The market overview shows a total of 35% leased vessels out of 160 total vessels, comprising 80 leased vessels and 80 owned vessels. The total TEUs are 1,300,000, with 800,000 leased TEUs and 500,000 owned TEUs, reflecting a 13% year-over-year change and a market share of 21%, generating $5 million in revenue and a profit margin of 3200%. In a further analysis, total containers are again 240, operational fleet stands at 150 with 90 leased containers. Here, market overview indicates a total of 38% leased vessels out of 170 total vessels, with 95 leased vessels and 700,000 TEUs generating $6 million in revenue and a profit margin of 3300%. The final row reflects a total of 260 containers, with 160 operational and 100 leased, showing 36% leased vessels and a total of 1,600,000 TEUs, contributing to a profit margin of 3300%.
